dataSources.name: Erik Voeten UNGA Voting Data
dataSources.description: Roll-call voting data for all UNGA resolutions 1946–2023. Provides ideal point estimates and per-resolution country votes.
dataSources.usage: Country ideal points, voting pattern baselines, historical calibration targets

dataSources.name: V-Dem (Varieties of Democracy) v14
dataSources.description: Democracy indicators for 202 countries, 1789–2023. Covers electoral, liberal, participatory, deliberative, and egalitarian dimensions.
dataSources.usage: Democracy index scores, regime classification, behavioral trait calibration

dataSources.name: UN Digital Library
dataSources.description: Official repository of UN documents, resolutions, and voting records.
dataSources.usage: Resolution texts, vote tallies for validation, historical precedents

dataSources.name: Security Council Veto List
dataSources.description: Complete list of vetoes cast in the Security Council since 1946.
dataSources.usage: Veto pattern analysis, P5 behavioral calibration

dataSources.name: World Bank Open Data
dataSources.description: Development indicators including GDP per capita, population, governance metrics.
dataSources.usage: Economic context for voting behavior modeling

positionComputation: Ideal Point Alignment (25%): Distance between country's Voeten ideal point and resolution's aggregate position
Policy Dimension Matching (30%): Weighted dot product across 6 dimensions (sovereignty, human rights, development, security, environment, decolonization)
Topic Voting History (20%): Historical voting rate on resolution's topic categories
Bloc Coordination (15%): Average predicted vote of bloc peers, weighted by bloc cohesion scores
Bilateral Relations (10%): Alliance and rivalry modifiers (planned for v2)
methodology.voteDecision: Softmax3 function converts composite score to probability distribution [P(Yes), P(No), P(Abstain)]. Argmax determines predicted vote. Abstain probability boosted for countries with weak position signals and lower democracy indices.
limitations: Current model uses static ideal points — does not capture year-over-year drift in positions
Bilateral relations and specific diplomatic disputes not yet modeled
Topic-specific voting history not yet populated from Voeten roll-call data
Resolution language analysis is approximate — actual clause wording matters
Does not model last-minute diplomatic pressure, side deals, or vote trading
Small island states with sparse voting records are less accurately predicted

results.title: Protection of civilians and upholding legal and humanitarian obligations (Gaza ceasefire)
results.notes: Emergency Special Session on Palestine. One of the largest Yes votes on a Palestine resolution. Demanded immediate humanitarian ceasefire.

results.title: Principles of the Charter of the United Nations underlying a comprehensive, just and lasting peace in Ukraine
results.notes: Western-aligned resolution condemning Russian aggression. Global South split: many abstained rather than opposing.

results.title: Convention on the prohibition of the use of nuclear weapons
results.notes: NATO + nuclear states vote No. Global South overwhelmingly Yes. Classic North-South divide on disarmament.

results.title: Security Council draft resolution demanding immediate humanitarian ceasefire in Gaza
results.notes: 13-1-1 vote with sole US veto. UK abstained. All other P5 (Russia, China, France) voted Yes.

results.title: Protection of global climate for present and future generations of humankind
results.notes: Near-consensus climate resolution. Only USA, Russia, Iran voted No. Oil states abstained.

results.title: The right to development
results.notes: Classic North-South divide. Western states oppose on grounds it implies economic obligations. G77 strongly supports.
